i063:
Founder · World Protocol Academy

ORCID 0009-0008-3219-394X
i065: “Protocol is not the decoration of diplomacy — it is its visible skeleton.”
i066: There are journals for diplomacy. There are journals for security. There are journals for communication. But protocol — as the operational language of the state — has remained dissolved among them, without its own academic home. The WPA Journal is born to occupy that place.
i067: In this inaugural issue (September 2026), we open the journal with a flagship paper (p. 09) that applies our own Feedback 360° methodology to the visits to China by Donald J. Trump and Vladimir V. Putin. Not as news — but as a structured corpus of protocol signals through which Beijing distributes dignity, warmth and strategic meaning.
i068: The paper's finding is simple but methodologically decisive: Trump's visits are characterised by controlled warmth — respect is visible, but proximity is bounded. Putin's visits are characterised by visible intimacy — repetition becomes institutional language. Both protocols are polite; they are not equal.
i069: This is the point of the WPA Journal: to read state visibility as a text. For medium-power states — and the Republic of North Macedonia is one — this is not a distant spectacle; it is a training field.
i074: Visits to China: Trump & Putin — A Feedback 360° Analysis
i075: Wrong Flag Display as a Protocol Breach
i082: The WPA Journal of Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication, Security & Communicology is a specialised international interdisciplinary journal published by the World Protocol Academy — an independent institute headquartered in Skopje.
i083: The mission is to be a unique academic and practitioner platform for ten converged domains: state protocol, diplomatic protocol, diplomacy and international relations, defence diplomacy, public communication and PR, crisis communication, communicology, security and strategic studies, documented practitioner experience, and protocol education.
i084: Doctrine: “Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.”
i085: The journal also develops its own methodological frameworks — among them Feedback 360° for state-visit analysis, applied in the flagship paper of this inaugural issue (p. 09).
i086: Ten article types are accepted: research, practitioner, case study, policy brief, protocol scenario, book review, methodology note, interview, comparative institutional note, and teaching note.
i092: Abstract — This paper treats the Beijing visits of Presidents Trump and Putin not as isolated diplomatic episodes but as a structured corpus of protocol signals through which China distributes dignity, warmth, distance and strategic meaning.
i095: Trump 2017/2026: respect is visible, dignity preserved, but proximity is deliberately bounded.
i098: Putin 2024–2026: repetition becomes institutional language; memory becomes protocol capital.
i099: Methodologically: Feedback 360° examines chronology, reception level, ceremonial location, delegation composition, signed documents, post-visit rhetoric and non-verbal micro-signals (handshake, touch, camera-facing orientation).
i103: The WPA Journal commits to the principle of accessibility without compromise of integrity. Submission, peer review, and editorial decision are free. A symbolic fee only after acceptance. Waivers available.
i108: Symbolic publication fee — only after acceptance
i110: No payment for acceptance. No payment for faster peer review. No hidden fees. Reviewers do not know waiver status.
i113: The WPA Journal explicitly welcomes authors and institutions from all 15 global regions — with special attention to those underrepresented in mainstream Western academic streams.
i114: Sub-region descriptors are accepted where applicable.
i121: As part of WPA’s planned global outreach to all 15 regions, the WPA Journal opens the call for Issue II (March 2027), which will accommodate additional submissions beyond the inaugural cycle.
i123: Protocol, Defence Diplomacy and National Security
i127: Ceremonial, Dress Code and Symbolic Authority
i129: Protocol Errors, Gaffes and Scenario Analysis
i131: Digital Protocol and AI-Assisted Institutional Communication
i133: High-quality submissions that cannot be accommodated in the inaugural issue (September 2026) due to time constraints will be carried forward with priority into Issue II (March 2027). Authors will be notified with a confirmation of prioritised review status.
i135: WPA Journal invites professionals in protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security and communicology to express interest in author contributions, reviewer roles, practitioner notes, interviews or future advisory cooperation.
i151: Prepare manuscript: B5/A4, 2.5 cm margins, Garamond 11–12pt, 1.5 spacing.
i152: Include abstract (200–300 words), 5–10 keywords, ORCID, AI usage + COI declarations.
i153: Send to email — confirmation and submission ID within 5 working days.
i154: Desk review 7–14 days · peer review 60–90 days · decision: accept / revisions / reject.
i155: Symbolic fee only after acceptance (waivers on request); production and archiving.

i162: Abstract — This paper typologises cases of incorrect flag display — reversed side, wrong order, wrong colour, missing or added symbolism — and treats them as measurable protocol violations with diplomatic and communication consequences.

i168: Abstract — Sixty-five years after entry into force, the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations (VCDR, 1961) remains a central reference, but its textual formulations are increasingly tested by digital diplomacy, hybrid threats and extended personae without classical diplomatic status.
i169: Keywords · VCDR · diplomatic law · international law · immunity · contemporary practice

i191: The five article-opening templates (Research Article, Policy Brief, Protocol Scenario Analysis, Comparative Institutional Note, Book Review) demonstrate the standardised WPA Journal frame: 30×40 mm author portrait, bibliographic metadata, and bilingual abstract. The full articles will be published in the inaugural issue (September 2026).
i195: The WPA does not claim indexing in Scopus, Web of Science, DOAJ, Google Scholar, Crossref, ORCID or AD Scientific Index. All markers are planned / intended / under development.
i196: Zenodo archival record planned after Issue 1 publication. A Zenodo DOI may be assigned to the issue record and/or to individual article records. DOI identifies a digital object, not the journal as a continuing serial publication.
i197: e-ISSN application planned through the national ISSN route / National and University Library system in the Republic of North Macedonia, when the journal has the required publication basis. If a print edition is later established, a separate print ISSN may be requested; ISSN-L may be considered later if both electronic and print formats exist. ISSN identifies the journal itself as a continuing resource.
i199: discoverability intended after publication, subject to crawler indexing — not claimed
i201: DOI and ISSN are not the same identifier. DOI will be used for archival records, individual articles or digital objects, while ISSN identifies the journal as a serial publication. WPA Journal plans Zenodo archiving and DOI records, as well as a separate ISSN application through the national ISSN procedure in the Republic of North Macedonia.
